hard.next_step: 6/19/26 MQ Alec notified me that we are one of the vendors qualified to participate in the RFP phase. RFP is slated to be issued by 7/10. 6/12/23 MQ RFI submitted. Qualified vendors will be notified of their inclusion in RFP phase. 6/3/26. MQ Initial call completed. Opportunity is Qualified. Renuity currently relies on manual processes across all procurement functions. They are a PE backed company that has recently acquired a variety of residential services based companies that span across the US. Procurement is decentralized. Alec has been tasked by COO to find an S2P solution that meets their requirements. Alec is inviting Zycus to participate in their RFI. Submissions are due 6/12. Based on responses, Renuity will select 3 vendors to participate in susequent RFP planned to be released 6/26.
hard.ais_why:
• Renuity's Alec Diaz is 'building the indirect procurement function from the ground up,' aiming to centralize decentralized P-card and manual processes ‚Äî foundational modernization, not an AI mandate.
• Agentic AI, Merlin and Merlin Intake are scoped and the deal is at Formal Evaluation via an RFI-to-RFP path, so AI could feature in selection.
• No agentic-platform discussion or AI sentiment is recorded yet, keeping appetite in the curious range.

north_star_verdict.headline: Early formal evaluation moving on pace: Zycus qualified into the RFP round, RFP slated to issue ~10 Jul, with a credible 31 Oct close 117 days out.
north_star_verdict.math: Created 3 Jun 2026, in Formal Evaluation ~33 days. RFI submitted 12 Jun; Zycus notified of RFP qualification 19 Jun. RFP was slated to issue by 10 Jul. Last genuine buyer touch was the incoming email 19 Jun 2026 (17 days ago); rep-side call-attempt tasks logged 23 Jun. Close 31 Oct is 117 days out; remaining milestones (RFP response, deeper demo, vendor down-select, business case, ELT approval, contracting) fit comfortably inside 117 days if cadence holds.
evidence: Next Step (SF, 19 Jun 2026): Zycus confirmed as one of the vendors qualified to participate in the RFP phase; RFP slated to issue by 10 Jul.
Buyer email received 19 Jun 2026 (Alec Diaz, incoming reply): 'Based on my review of your RFI response, I plan to move your organization forward.'
Intro call 3 Jun 2026 (Alec Diaz): 'I plan a short list that for the RFP process and then the RFP the week after July 4.'

metrics.narrative: Alec Diaz framed the value qualitatively but no dollar figure is quantified yet. On the 3 Jun 2026 intro call he described indirect spend as fully decentralized: 'everything's on p-cards, on personal cards, where we get random invoices sent into our e-AP email, that's manually processed,' with 'really no national account for indirect spend.' Marc Quessenberry tied ROI to consolidating that Maverick spend ('it makes my ROI much easier... the ROI is gonna be paying for the system within a year or two'), but neither party put numbers to current spend, a savings target, or headcount cost. Quantifying the addressable indirect spend and a target savings percentage is the move, and it is exactly what Alec needs to build the business case he must present to the ELT.
sources: Avoma intro call 3 Jun 2026 (Alec Diaz, Marc Quessenberry)

economic_buyer.narrative: The economic decision sits above Alec Diaz with the COO and the ELT, and Alec laid out the approval path on the 3 Jun 2026 call: the COO tasked him to 'do an RFP... vet it down, obviously, into one vendor and kinda come up with the business case and ROI, present it out to the ELT, and then get the buy in and approval from them.' So budget/approval authority is identified by ROLE (COO sponsor, ELT approver) but neither the COO nor any ELT member is named in Salesforce, has joined a call, or has been engaged. Alec runs the evaluation but does not control the budget. The move is to get the COO named and sponsoring before the business case lands, since the deal is won or lost on ELT buy-in Alec does not yet own.

decision_criteria.narrative: Criteria are emerging from one discovery call and an RFI Alec drafted, not yet a formal scored list. Alec named his target functional scope directly: 'eProcurement, spend analysis, prior management, and eSourcing... and then also AI capabilities as well,' calling it 'everything that I'm really looking for in a tool.' He probed specifically on Microsoft Teams-based conversational intake and on punch-out catalogs pulling in additional vendors, signaling intake/orchestration and ease-of-use matter to him, and cited Gartner positioning as the reason he engaged ('I saw you guys on Gartner'). The full weighted criteria will surface in the RFP document expected ~10 Jul; obtaining that RFP and mapping the Zycus response to its scored criteria is the criteria-confirmation move.

decision_process.narrative: The buyer's process is explicit and well-mapped from the 3 Jun 2026 call and the SF Next Step trail: RFI issued (responses due 12 Jun) then shortlist 3 vendors, issue the RFP the week after 4 July (updated to slated for 10 Jul, confirmed 19 Jun), deeper demo with a prior solution-consultant discovery session, vet to a single vendor, build the business case and ROI, present to the ELT for buy-in and approval, then contracting. Zycus cleared the first gate, confirmed 19 Jun 2026 as a qualified RFP vendor. The chief unknown is the internal ELT approval mechanics (who sits on it, how many approvers, what bar), which sits entirely behind Alec.
sources: SF Next Step notes 3 Jun / 12 Jun / 19 Jun 2026

paper_process.narrative: Contracting mechanics are not yet discussed, which is appropriate this early in Formal Evaluation, but the paper trail has actually started: an MNDA was exchanged and signed in the first week (Alec sent Renuity's MNDA template 5 Jun, Marc returned a signed copy 9 Jun), and Alec confirmed his own legal must template any agreement before signature ('I will need the information first so legal can put it into the template'). Renuity runs Acumatica as its ERP (Alec, 3 Jun 2026), so an integration and InfoSec review will eventually be in scope. There is no signal yet on MSA/SOW, procurement channel, or security review. The move is to surface the contracting and security path during the RFP/demo phase so it does not compress the timeline near the 31 Oct close.

identify_pain.narrative: The operational pain is concrete and owned by Alec Diaz, the sole person standing up indirect procurement at a PE-backed roll-up that has grown 'tremendously over the last 6 to 7 years through our acquisitions.' On 3 Jun 2026 he stated procurement is 'very decentralized. Everything's on p-cards, on personal cards, where we get random invoices sent into our e-AP email, that's manually processed,' with 'really no national account for indirect spend' and no controls on branch-level Maverick spend. He is tasked with consolidating all indirect suppliers and building the function 'from the ground up,' reporting to the COO. This is a clear, confirmed pain with a named owner; the only gap is quantification (see Metrics), not whether the pain exists.

champion.narrative: Alec Diaz is a developing champion: he is the Senior Manager of Indirect Procurement, owns the evaluation, invited Zycus into the RFI, and was visibly engaged on the 3 Jun 2026 call, asking detailed product questions on Teams-based intake, punch-out catalogs, and approval workflows. What has developed is genuine product interest and control of the process, and he moved Zycus forward into the RFP round on 19 Jun 2026. What is still missing is proof of advocacy for Zycus specifically (he was transparent he is 'looking at other solutions as well') and any line to the ELT who hold the budget. He reports to the COO but does not control spend, so he is a coach/buyer-side champion who needs arming with the quantified business case and an executive introduction to become decisive.

competition.narrative: This is an openly competitive multi-vendor RFI/RFP. Alec Diaz said directly on the 3 Jun 2026 call: 'to be transparent, obviously, looking at other solutions as well,' and plans to shortlist 3 vendors for the RFP. No specific rival was named in any evidence read this sweep, so the competitor set remains unnamed. Zycus's differentiation wedge from the call is the single-code-base, AI-native suite (Merlin intake/orchestration in Teams, Anna autonomous negotiation, AP Smartdesk, iRisk) versus rivals who 'go out and buy a solution from someone else, then try to integrate that... and they have nightmares with the integration piece.' Winning requires landing that AI and ease-of-integration story hard in the RFP and demo; the move is to get the other shortlisted vendor names so positioning can be sharpened.
sources: Avoma intro call 3 Jun 2026 (Alec Diaz, Marc Quessenberry)

deal_movement.summary: A newly created, fast-moving early-stage deal: qualified 4 Jun, RFI submitted 12 Jun, and Zycus down-selected into the RFP round on 19 Jun. Last genuine two-way buyer exchange was the 19 Jun incoming email; the 23 Jun records are rep-side call-attempt tasks, so the deal is live and awaiting the RFP issuance (~10 Jul).

items.change: Stage advanced Qualified to Formal Evaluation
Zycus qualified into the RFP phase per Alec Diaz email
MNDA signed and returned by Zycus

competitive_position.summary: Openly competitive multi-vendor RFI/RFP with 3 vendors to be shortlisted for the RFP; the rival names are not disclosed in any evidence yet. The single current threat is being out-positioned on AI/ease-of-use or price during the RFP and demo rather than any named incumbent. Zycus's wedge is its single-code-base, AI-native suite (Merlin intake in Teams, Anna negotiation agent, AP Smartdesk, iRisk) versus competitors who bolt on acquired modules and 'have nightmares with the integration piece.'

competitors.how_we_win: Land the single-code-base AI story (Merlin intake in Teams, Anna autonomous negotiation, AP Smartdesk) and contrast clean native integration vs competitors' bolt-on integration pain during the RFP and demo; make AI the Phase 1 centerpiece for a PE-backed decentralized roll-up.

customer_expectations_fit.summary: Strong functional-scope alignment from the intro call: Alec's stated wish-list (eProcurement, spend analysis, supplier management, eSourcing, plus AI) maps directly onto the scoped Zycus suite, and he is drawn to Teams-based intake. Integration with Acumatica (a mid-tier ERP) is the one item still to be validated.

best_practice_check.summary: Win-strategy levers for an early, single-threaded, competitive RFP where budget authority sits above the champion.
flags: Multi-thread beyond Alec into the COO sponsor and IT/Acumatica owner before RFP scoring locks; today the deal is single-threaded on one manager.
Quantify Renuity's indirect spend baseline and a target savings figure to arm Alec's ELT business case, converting qualitative pain into an ROI he can defend.
Get the other shortlisted vendor names so the RFP response leads with the single-code-base AI wedge against their specific weaknesses.

items.name: Alec Diaz
items.title: Senior Manager, Indirect Procurement
items.risk: Owns the evaluation and moved us into the RFP round, but does not control budget and is openly evaluating alternatives; advocacy for Zycus specifically is not yet proven.
items.source: SF task/activity contact + email 19 Jun 2026; Avoma intro call 3 Jun 2026

champion_strength.summary: Alec Diaz owns the evaluation, invited Zycus in, engaged deeply on product detail, and advanced Zycus into the RFP round on 19 Jun, but is openly evaluating alternatives, does not control budget, and his advocacy for Zycus specifically is still unproven.
champion_strength.source: Avoma intro call 3 Jun 2026 (Alec Diaz); SF email 19 Jun 2026

ai_positioning_strength.summary: Zycus led the intro call heavily on its AI-native story (Merlin intake in Teams, Anna autonomous negotiation, AP Smartdesk, iRisk, 32 AI patents), and Alec named AI as part of what he wants, but no buyer AI sentiment or agentic-platform discussion is recorded yet, so the AI narrative is delivered but not yet validated by the buyer.

ai_fit_signal.summary: AIS field reads 5.0 / Moderate: foundational modernization of a from-scratch indirect function rather than an AI mandate. Alec did name 'AI capabilities as well' as something he is looking for, but expressed no strong agentic appetite on the one call, keeping the signal curious rather than hungry.

items.detail: COO sponsor and ELT approvers control budget but are unnamed and unengaged; the deal is won or lost on ELT buy-in Alec does not own.
Single-threaded on Alec Diaz; no second buyer-side contact engaged, so the deal rests on one manager's advocacy.
No indirect spend baseline or savings figure quantified, so the ROI Alec must take to the ELT is not yet built.
Competitive 3-vendor RFP with rivals unnamed; risk of being out-positioned on AI, ease-of-use, or price in the RFP and demo.

confidence_signals.summary: Deal is live and engaged: Alec moved Zycus into the RFP round on 19 Jun and the last two-way exchange was 17 days ago, consistent with awaiting the ~10 Jul RFP issuance. Not cooling, but engagement rests on a single contact.
items: Buyer advanced Zycus into the RFP round on 19 Jun 2026.
Only one buyer contact (Alec Diaz) engaged to date.
Awaiting buyer-owed RFP document slated for ~10 Jul 2026.

critical_signals.text: The 3 Jun intro call landed well: Alec engaged deeply on Teams-based intake and approval workflows and then moved Zycus into the RFP round on 19 Jun.
It is an open 3-vendor RFP and Alec confirmed he is evaluating other solutions, but the rival names are not yet disclosed.
The COO sponsor and the ELT approvers hold the budget and final sign-off, yet only Alec has been met so far.
No indirect spend baseline or savings target is quantified yet, so the ROI Alec needs for the ELT is still unbuilt.

deal_scores_evidence.summary: We cleared the first gate into Renuity's competitive 3-vendor RFP with a genuinely engaged champion in Alec Diaz, but budget authority sits with an unnamed COO and ELT we have never met and the ROI case is still unquantified.

win_position.text: We are early but well-placed: Alec Diaz advanced Zycus into the RFP round on 19 Jun and his stated scope (eProcurement, spend analysis, supplier management, eSourcing, plus AI) maps directly onto the scoped suite, which he called 'everything that I'm really looking for in a tool' (Avoma, 3 Jun).
The economic decision sits with a COO and ELT who are unnamed and have never joined a call, so the deal is won or lost on approvals Alec does not control (Avoma, 3 Jun).
It is an openly competitive 3-vendor RFP: Alec said he is 'looking at other solutions as well,' and the rivals are still unnamed, so our RFP positioning is being sharpened blind (Avoma, 3 Jun).

deal_momentum.text: Fast early cadence: qualified 4 Jun, RFI submitted 12 Jun, and down-selected into the RFP round by 19 Jun (SF Next Step, 19 Jun).
The buyer set concrete dates himself, planning the RFP for the week after 4 July, now slated for ~10 Jul (SF Next Step, 19 Jun).
The last genuine two-way exchange was the 19 Jun email; the 23 Jun records are rep-side call attempts, so momentum now hinges on the buyer-owed RFP arriving on time.

customer_commitment.text: Alec signed and exchanged an MNDA in the first week (returned 9 Jun) and formally notified Zycus of RFP qualification, both real commitment signals (SF tasks, 5-19 Jun).
Commitment is confined to one manager: no COO/ELT sponsor is engaged and no budget or savings figure has been shared, so there is no executive-level commitment yet (Avoma, 3 Jun).

deal_risk.text: Single-threaded on Alec Diaz with no second buyer contact, so the deal rests entirely on one person's advocacy (SF contact roles).
The ROI Alec must present to the ELT is unbuilt because indirect spend is unquantified, leaving the business case exposed at the approval gate (Avoma, 3 Jun).

executive_alignment.evidence: COO sponsor and ELT approvers unnamed and unengaged; single-threaded on Alec Diaz (Avoma, 3 Jun).

business_case.evidence: Pain is clear but no spend baseline or savings figure quantified (Avoma, 3 Jun).

customer_preference.evidence: Alec asked deep Zycus-specific product questions (Teams intake, punch-outs, approval flows) and called the scope 'everything I'm really looking for,' but was transparent he is 'looking at other solutions as well' with no decisive lean (Avoma, 3 Jun).

business_case.evidence: Qualitative pain only: fully decentralized P-card/manual indirect spend with no national account, but no spend baseline, savings target, or ROI figure quantified (Avoma, 3 Jun).

items.expected_effect: Converts qualitative pain into a defensible ROI Alec can carry to the ELT, strengthening our RFP case.

contributions.evidence: Next Step carries 6 dated milestone(s) — actively worked.

commentary.win_position: Win 44 is carried by momentum 59 vs stage-expected 52; preference strength +0.40 (weight 20). Offsetting it: differentiation strength -0.40 (weight 20).
commentary.deal_momentum: Momentum 59 (moving forward): Next Step carries 6 dated milestone(s) — actively worked. Silence alone was not treated as backward motion.
commentary.customer_commitment: Commitment sits near the earned-from-zero floor: little observable customer investment surfaced. Commitment is earned, so a low value is honest, not a penalty.
commentary.deal_risk: Risk 0: no break-risk was observed in the swept evidence. Coverage is thin, so read this as 'nothing seen yet,' not 'nothing there.'
commentary.forecast_confidence: Forecast confidence 36 is anchored on the stage win (44), nudged by commitment 8 and momentum 59, and dragged by risk 0. Read is Early Read — a confidence flag (we know less), not a haircut on the score.
commentary.evidence_coverage: Early Read: 2 of 7 evidence dimensions seen (stage integrity, forward motion). It labels how much of the picture we have, never the primary scores.
